data.title: Zumiez anticipates 3%–4% sales growth in 2025 while targeting higher private label penetration and margin improvement
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zumiez Inc. (ZUMZ) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Richard Brooks reported that "our second quarter results exceeded expectations, demonstrating the continued resilience of our North American business and the effectiveness of our strategic initiatives." Brooks highlighted a 2.5% increase in comparable sales, marking the fifth consecutive quarter of positive comparable sales growth, and noted an accelerating two-year comparable sales stack. He said, "Our momentum continued to build into August with low teens comparable sales growth in the United States on top of a double-digit increase in the year-ago period, providing confidence in our approach and optimism heading into the important holiday season.”

  • Brooks identified three strategic priorities: driving revenue growth through customer-focused initiatives, sustaining profitability optimization, and capitalizing on the company’s strong financial foundation to manage volatility and fund expansion. He stressed the expansion of private label, stating, "Private label performance remains exceptionally strong, reaching 30% of total sales year-to-date through the second quarter versus 27% a year ago and representing the highest private label penetration in our history."

  • CFO Christopher Work stated, "Second quarter net sales were $214.3 million, up 1.9% from $210.2 million in the second quarter of 2024. Comparable sales were up 2.5% for the quarter. As Rick mentioned, the primary driver was our North America business, which shows outsized strength even as macroeconomic uncertainty spurred by global trade policy intensified during the period."

Outlook

  • Work reported, "We are anticipating total sales will be between $232 million and $237 million for the 13 weeks ended November 1, 2025, including comparable sales growth of 5.5% to 7.5% over the prior year."

  • For Q3, product margin is expected to increase from the same period last year, and operating income is projected to be between 2.3% and 3.3% of sales. Expected earnings per share for Q3 are "between $0.19 and $0.29 compared to EPS of $0.06 in the prior year."

  • For the full year 2025, management reaffirmed sales growth expectations of 3% to 4%, modest product margin improvement, and a year-over-year increase in operating margins and net profit. Store closures are expected to have a negative impact on sales of roughly $14 million for the year, with six new store openings planned and around 20 closures.

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q2 were $214.3 million. Gross profit reached $76 million, representing 35.5% of sales versus 34.2% the prior year. Net loss for the quarter was $1 million, or $0.06 per share, compared to a net loss of $0.8 million, or $0.04 per share, in Q2 2024.

  • Inventory was $157.7 million, down 0.6% year-over-year. Cash and current marketable securities totaled $106.7 million at quarter end, with no debt. The company repurchased 0.6 million shares for $7.8 million during the quarter.

  • Third quarter-to-date, net sales for the 30-day period ended September 1, 2025, increased 10.6% year-over-year, with comparable sales up 11.2%. North America net sales increased 11.7%, while other international rose 2.3%.

Q&A

  • Mitchel Kummetz, Seaport Research Partners, asked about comp assumptions for the balance of Q3 and operating margin breakdown. CFO Work responded that "as we've seen from the last couple of years, we have seen kind of the nonpeak time periods slow...we are assuming a much lower comp level in the, I'll say, low single digits...from here on out, we would assume it to go back to something closer to what we saw in Q2."

  • Kummetz inquired about drivers behind average unit retail (AUR) increases. CEO Brooks explained that pricing actions, mix shift, and private label initiatives all contributed, and noted, "the price we took was also in our private label businesses."

  • Kummetz asked about private label penetration by category, specifically denim. Brooks replied, "you can be in our stores and see how significant our denim presentation is amongst our private label brands...it's been, again, reflective of what's going on in the consumer world."

  • Jeff Van Sinderen, B. Riley Securities, asked about long-term operating margin potential. CFO Work stated, "over time, we still believe we can get long-term operating margins back to that high single-digit level."

  • Van Sinderen asked about European business headwinds. Brooks said, "we need the economy to cooperate there...our largest market for us in Europe is Germany, which the last years has had basically no growth."

  • Kummetz followed up on skate and footwear categories. Brooks responded, "our skate business -- skate hardgoods business has turned positive. Now we're still going to be cautious...Shoes continue to be...a very challenging category overall."

  • Kummetz also asked about product margin delta between private label and third party. CFO Work estimated "probably ten to 15% benefit in our private label product from our pure branded product."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral, with detailed questions about comp trends, category drivers, and margin dynamics.

  • Management was confident in prepared remarks, using phrases like "I am confident in our ability to generate value for our entire stakeholder community," but expressed measured caution about nonpeak periods and international challenges.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts maintained a cautious but constructive outlook. The frequency of questions about category dynamics and operating leverage increased, reflecting heightened focus on profitability drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q3 now includes a specific range for sales and EPS, compared to a more cautious and broader range in Q1. Full year sales growth expectations remain at 3% to 4%.

  • Private label penetration increased from 30% in Q1 to 30% year-to-date in Q2, with management emphasizing its margin contribution.

  • Operating loss narrowed from Q1 to Q2, with stronger gross margin improvement (up to 35.5% vs. 30% in Q1). North America continues to drive growth, while international headwinds persist.

  • Analysts' focus shifted toward sustainability of comps, drivers of AUR, and product margin potential, with continued scrutiny of European performance.

  • Management’s confidence level remained steady, highlighting resilience and adaptability, but continued to signal caution regarding international business and tariffs.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited macroeconomic uncertainty, tariffs, and consumer discretionary pressure as ongoing risks.

  • European market conditions remain challenging, with Brooks stating, "market conditions are challenging, making it difficult to build on the improvements we made in sales, product margin operating results in 2024."

  • Store closures are expected to negatively impact annual sales by about $14 million.

  • Analysts probed for clarity on the sustainability of recent comp gains and the outlook for international recovery.

Final Takeaway

Zumiez management emphasized that its North American business continues to outperform expectations, with private label reaching a record share of sales and driving margin improvement. The company remains focused on customer-centric initiatives, premium pricing, and disciplined expense management to navigate ongoing economic and trade-related uncertainties. Management reiterated its confidence in achieving 3% to 4% sales growth for 2025, underpinned by strong back-to-school trends and continued margin expansion, while acknowledging headwinds in international markets and ongoing risks from tariffs and store closures.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Oklo plans $1.7B Tennessee plant to recycle nuclear waste to fuel
data.description:
Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) said Thursday it plans to design, build and operate a facility in Tennessee to convert nuclear waste into fuel to power its own advanced small modular reactors, a first-of-its-kind recycling plant in the U.S. it expects to cost $1.68B.

Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) said it has already completed a licensing project for the facility with the Nuclear Regulatory Commission, and is in the pre-application stage for bringing it into operation; it anticipates the plant will begin producing fuel for its SMRs by the early 2030s.

The company also said it is in talks with Tennessee Valley Authority to recycle waste from the utility's nuclear plants, and possibly to sell it electricity from its planned Aurora Powerhouse reactor.

More than 94K metric tons of used nuclear fuel are stored at U.S. power plants that could be recycled, and doing so would unlock the energy equivalent of 1.3T barrels of oil, the company said.

"Fuel is the single most important factor in bringing advanced nuclear energy to market," Oklo (OKLO) CEO Jacob DeWitte said. "By recycling used nuclear fuel at an industrial scale, we are turning waste into watts, cutting costs, and establishing a secure U.S. supply chain."

data.title: LGI Homes Closes 372 homes in August 2025
data.description:
• LGI Homes (NASDAQ:LGIH) said on Thursday it closed 372 homes in August 2025, including 14 single-family rental homes that are currently or were previously leased.
  • As of August 31, the company had 142 active selling communities.
  • LGIH +0.21% after hours to $65.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Brookfield Asset Management prices $750 mln senior notes due 2055
data.description:
• Brookfield Asset Management (TSX:BAM:CA) on Thursday announced it has priced a public offering of $750 million in senior notes due 2055, with an annual interest rate of 6.077%.
  • Proceeds from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on September 9, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • The notes are being issued under Brookfield’s existing base shelf prospectus in the U.S. and Canada, and pursuant to an effective registration statement filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sweetgreen CFO Mitch Reback to retire; Jamie McConnell named successor
data.description:
• Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG) said on Thursday that Chief Financial Officer Mitch Reback will retire on September 21, 2025, and will be succeeded by Jamie McConnell, effective September 22, 2025.

  • Reback, who joined Sweetgreen in 2015, played a key role in building the company’s financial infrastructure and guiding it through its transition to a public company. He will remain in an advisory role for six months to support the leadership transition.
  • McConnell brings over 20 years of financial leadership experience, most recently serving as Chief Accounting and Administrative Officer at Chipotle.
  • She has also held senior roles at Aviation Capital Group, Rent-A-Center, Allergan, and Deloitte.
  • SG +0.22% after hours to $8.76
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Great Elm Capital prices public offering of $50 million of 7.75% notes due 2030
data.description:
• Great Elm Capital (GECC) announced on Thursday the pricing of its underwritten public offering of $50 million aggregate principal amount of its 7.75% notes due 2030, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $48.1 million after payment of underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by the Company.
  • The Notes will mature on December 31, 2030, and may be redeemed in whole or in part at any time or from time to time at the company’s option on or after December 31, 2027.
  • The company has also granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $7.5 million aggregate principal amount of Notes to cover over-allotments, if any.
  • The closing of the transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, and the Notes are expected to be delivered on or about September 11, 2025.
  • The Notes are expected to be listed on The Nasdaq Global Market under the trading symbol “GECCG,” and to trade thereon within 30 days from the original issue date.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from the offering to redeem all of its outstanding 8.75% notes due 2028 and the remainder of the proceeds may be used to redeem or repurchase all or a portion of its outstanding 5.875% notes due 2026 and to repurchase all or a portion of its outstanding 8.50% notes due 2029 as well as for the repurchase of all or a portion of its outstanding 8.125% notes due 2029 and to repay all or a portion of any borrowings outstanding under its revolving credit facility or for general corporate purposes, including making investments consistent with its investment objectives.
  • GEG -7.64% after hours to $2.7706.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mirion Technologies set to join S&P SmallCap 600
data.description:
• Mirion Technologies (NYSE:MIR) will replace GMS (NYSE:GMS) in the S&P SmallCap 600 effective before the opening of trading on Tuesday, September 9.
  • S&P 500 and 100 constituent Home Depot (HD) acquired GMS in a deal that was completed today.
  • MIR stock up over 6% in extended trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump signs Japan trade pact, capping tariffs at 15%
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an order to implement the U.S.-Japan trade deal reached in July.

The move closes months of back-and-forth with Japan on deal terms, locking in a maximum 15% tariff on most Japanese goods. U.S. 

Most goods shipped after Aug. 7 will face the confirmed 15% duty, according to the order.

Meanwhile, tariff breaks for aerospace and auto imports kick in a week from Thursday.

And, as part of the agreement, the U.S. will roll back some duties on aircraft and parts, along with generic drugs, ingredients and precursor chemicals, the White House said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Gold could hit $5,000 next year if Fed independence threatened, Goldman Sachs says
data.description:
The gold price is poised to reach $5,000/oz next year if the independence of the U.S. Federal Reserve is damaged and investors shift just 1% of their holdings from Treasurys into bullion, Goldman Sachs said Thursday.

"A scenario where Fed independence is damaged would likely lead to higher inflation, lower stock and long-dated bond prices, and an erosion of the dollar's reserve currency status," while gold is "a store of value that doesn't rely on international trust," Goldman analysts including Samantha Dart said in a note.

Goldman outlined a range of possible outcomes for the yellow metal, with a baseline forecast for a rise to $4,000/oz by mid-2026, a "tail-risk" scenario of $4,500/oz, and an estimate of nearly $5,000/oz if just 1% of the privately-owned U.S. Treasury market flows into gold.

"As a result, gold remains our highest-conviction long recommendation," Dart and her team wrote.

Analysts at J.P. Morgan also pointed to a potential erosion of Fed independence in its forecast for gold to potentially hit $4,250/oz by the end of 2026.

The recent surge in the gold price - up 6.5% in six straight days of gains - followed President Trump's move to oust Fed governor Lisa Cook and replace her with a rate cut supporter.

On Thursday, gold futures fell modestly as traders took profits following the rally that lifted the metal to new all-time highs, with the focus now shifting to the U.S. payrolls report for fresh hints on the Fed's policy path and setting the tone for this month's Fed meeting.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for September delivery closed -0.7% to $3,565.80/oz, still the metal's second highest settlement in history, and front-month Comex September silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished -1.5% to $40.911/oz, snapping a five-session winning streak but still its third-highest settlement this year.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)
